Projects

Choose the project that matches the workflow pressure you need to fix first.

Each Sonicverse project solves one operational job cleanly and can stand alone or connect into a broader platform rollout over time.

Compare all projectsScannable by workflow pressure, team fit, and operational outcome.
Streaming Infrastructure
AS
Audio Streaming StackView

A self-hostable delivery stack for broadcasters and audio products that need reliable ingest, failover, adaptive output, and operational visibility.

Get to a production-ready streaming baseline in days instead of months.
Developer API
MM
Media Metadata APIView

A unified API for reading, writing, and normalizing metadata across ingest, archive, and distribution workflows.

Stop maintaining one-off parsers and make metadata flows predictable.
Planning & Operations
BS
Broadcast SchedulerView

An open scheduling engine for live and automated radio playout that keeps planners, operators, and integrations aligned.

Replace brittle scheduling workflows with a system designed for modern broadcasting.

Projects

Read the Sonicverse lineup like a technical brief, then open the project that fits first.

The projects overview is designed for fast comparison: clear buyer, clear outcome, and a visible first implementation boundary before anyone commits to a demo.

Compare projectsBook a demo
Three projects. Three distinct starting points.

Start with the workflow layer creating the most drag now, then expand into a broader platform over time.

Project mapPick the first implementation boundary, then go deeper.

The overview stays wide and scannable so teams can compare audience, outcome, and technical signal in one pass.

01
Streaming infrastructureAudio Streaming Stack

Best when uptime, failover, and listener delivery are the immediate risk.

For broadcasters and live audio teams
02
Developer APIMedia Metadata API

Best when metadata complexity is slowing archives, feeds, or publishing workflows.

For platform and archive teams
03
Planning and operationsBroadcast Scheduler

Best when schedules, overrides, and fallback programming are hard to coordinate.

For stations and operations teams

Lineup

Compare the project lineup by use case, buyer, and operational outcome.

Each row is meant to answer the same three questions quickly: who is it for, what operational pressure does it reduce, and what signal tells you it is the right first move?

Need help choosing? Contact us
01Streaming Infrastructure

Audio Streaming Stack

A self-hostable delivery stack for broadcasters and audio products that need reliable ingest, failover, adaptive output, and operational visibility.

Best forBroadcasters, community radio, and platform teams
Main outcomeGet to a production-ready streaming baseline in days instead of months.
Delivery modesIcecast + HLS
Open project
02Developer API

Media Metadata API

A unified API for reading, writing, and normalizing metadata across ingest, archive, and distribution workflows.

Best forPodcast platforms, archive teams, and media engineering groups
Main outcomeStop maintaining one-off parsers and make metadata flows predictable.
Formats coveredID3, Vorbis, MP4, RSS
Open project
03Planning & Operations

Broadcast Scheduler

An open scheduling engine for live and automated radio playout that keeps planners, operators, and integrations aligned.

Best forCommunity stations, podcast networks, and audio operations teams
Main outcomeReplace brittle scheduling workflows with a system designed for modern broadcasting.
Operational modeLive + automated
Open project

Choosing a starting point

Most teams do not need the whole platform first. They need the right project first.

The overview is built to route teams by the operational bottleneck they already feel, not by abstract platform ambition.

Typical starting paths

Start with delivery if uptime is the risk, with metadata if publishing consistency is the bottleneck, or with scheduling if coordination and continuity are the pain.

01

If uptime is the issue

Start with Audio Streaming Stack when live distribution, failover, and playback reliability are the highest priorities.

02

If metadata drift is the issue

Start with Media Metadata API when multiple systems disagree on fields, formats, or publishing rules.

03

If coordination is the issue

Start with Broadcast Scheduler when schedules, overrides, and fallback programming are hard to coordinate.

Need the map in context?

We can walk through your current stack and point to the right first project.

That conversation works whether you are replacing one workflow now or planning a broader infrastructure refresh over time.

Book a project walkthroughContact sales
SonicverseAudio operations platform

Sonicverse is the modular audio operations platform for teams that want enterprise-grade workflows without proprietary lock-in.
